title: My pen pal in Kerala I have never met
summary: Reflect on a long friendship by letter with someone in another country whom you have never met in person.
topic: A long friendship across distance with someone you have never met
prompt: Write 9–11 sentences reflecting on a friendship with someone far away whom you may have never met in person (a pen pal, an online friend, a distant relative). Explain how it started and why it still matters. Use present perfect ('we have written'), past simple, and connectors.
hintStarters: We started writing to each other when we were both…
I have never seen her face, but I feel like I know…
Her letters always smell of…, and they tell me about a life that…
Even though we live thousands of kilometres apart, we…
sampleText: Anjali and I started writing to each other when we were both thirteen. A teacher gave us the names of students abroad, and I picked a girl in Kerala because I liked her town's name. That was sixteen years ago. We have written to each other ever since, mostly on paper, and I have kept every letter in a box. I have never seen her face in real life, and we have never met in person. Still, I feel like I know her better than some people I see every week. Her letters always smell faintly of the paper she uses, and they tell me about a life very different from mine. She writes about the monsoon, about her grandmother's garden, and about exams I will never take. When my father died, she was the first person I wrote to, even before some of my own family. Her reply came three weeks later, but it was the kindest thing I read that month. We do not write every week now, because we are both busy adults, but we stay in touch. Sometimes months pass with no letter, and then a thick envelope arrives. Even though we live thousands of kilometres apart, the distance has never felt like a wall. I do not need to meet her to know that she is my friend.

tên của các học sinh ở nước ngoài

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present perfect for an unfinished connection
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'We have written to each other ever since' và 'I have kept every letter' dùng hiện tại hoàn thành vì tình bạn bắt đầu trong quá khứ và vẫn tiếp tục đến nay. So với 'started writing... when we were thirteen' (quá khứ đơn cho thời điểm bắt đầu cụ thể).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'We have written to each other ever since' and 'I have kept every letter' use present perfect because the friendship began in the past and still continues. Compare 'started writing... when we were thirteen' (past simple for the specific starting point).

sampleGrammarNotes.title: even though for contrast
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'Even though we live thousands of kilometres apart, the distance has never felt like a wall' dùng 'even though' để nối hai ý trái ngược: ở xa nhau, nhưng vẫn gần gũi.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Even though we live thousands of kilometres apart, the distance has never felt like a wall' uses 'even though' to join two contrasting ideas: being far apart, yet still close.

sampleTranslationVi: Anjali và tôi bắt đầu viết thư cho nhau khi cả hai mười ba tuổi. Một thầy giáo đưa cho chúng tôi tên của các học sinh ở nước ngoài, và tôi chọn một cô bé ở Kerala vì tôi thích tên thị trấn của cô. Đó là mười sáu năm trước. Chúng tôi đã viết thư cho nhau từ đó đến nay, chủ yếu trên giấy, và tôi đã giữ mọi lá thư trong một chiếc hộp. Tôi chưa bao giờ thấy mặt cô ngoài đời thật, và chúng tôi chưa bao giờ gặp nhau trực tiếp. Vậy mà tôi cảm thấy mình hiểu cô hơn vài người tôi gặp mỗi tuần. Thư của cô luôn thoảng mùi giấy cô dùng, và chúng kể cho tôi về một cuộc sống rất khác của tôi. Cô viết về mùa mưa, về khu vườn của bà cô, và về những kỳ thi mà tôi sẽ không bao giờ trải qua. Khi cha tôi mất, cô là người đầu tiên tôi viết thư cho, thậm chí trước cả vài người trong gia đình tôi. Thư hồi đáp của cô đến ba tuần sau, nhưng đó là điều tử tế nhất tôi đọc trong tháng đó. Chúng tôi không viết mỗi tuần nữa, vì cả hai đều là người lớn bận rộn, nhưng chúng tôi giữ liên lạc. Đôi khi nhiều tháng trôi qua không có thư, rồi một phong bì dày đến. Mặc dù chúng tôi sống cách nhau hàng nghìn ki-lô-mét, khoảng cách chưa bao giờ giống như một bức tường. Tôi không cần gặp cô để biết rằng cô là bạn tôi.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best-written sentence about the ongoing letters.
options: We have written to each other ever since.
We have wrote to each other ever since.
We have written to each other since ever.
We are writing to each other ever since.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect 'have written' with 'ever since' shows a continuing connection. 'Have wrote' is wrong; 'since ever' is not idiomatic; 'are writing' loses the long span.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past participle of 'write'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that correctly says they have not met face to face.
options: We have never meet in person.
We have never met in person.
We never have met in person.
We have never met in the person.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Have never met in person' uses the past participle and the fixed phrase 'in person'. 'Meet' is wrong; word order in the third option is awkward; 'in the person' is incorrect.
mcQuestions.hint: Is the correct phrase 'in person' or 'in the person'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence states the start of the friendship in past simple?
options: We have started writing when we were both thirteen.
We started to writing when we were both thirteen.
We started writing when we were both thirteen.
We started writing when we are both thirteen.
mcQuestions.explanation: A specific past start uses past simple 'started writing... were'. Present perfect with a fixed time is wrong; 'to writing' is wrong; 'are' mismatches the past.
mcQuestions.hint: With a clear past time, which tense fits 'started'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that uses 'even though' for contrast correctly.
options: Even though we live far apart, but we stay close.
Even we live far apart though, we stay close.
Even though we living far apart, we stay close.
Even though we live far apart, we stay close.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Even though + clause' needs no extra 'but'. The second adds a redundant 'but'; the third scrambles word order; 'living' lacks an auxiliary.
mcQuestions.hint: Can you use 'even though' and 'but' in the same sentence?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural sentence about keeping the letters.
options: I have kept every letter in a box.
I have keep every letter in a box.
I have kept every letters in a box.
I have kept all letter in a box.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Have kept' (past participle) with singular 'every letter' is correct. 'Keep' is wrong; 'every letters' is ungrammatical; 'all letter' needs a plural.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'every' take a singular or plural noun?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the smell of the letters naturally?
options: Her letters always smell faintly the paper she uses.
Her letters always smell faintly of the paper she uses.
Her letters always smell faint of the paper she uses.
Her letters always smells faintly of the paper she uses.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Smell of something' needs the preposition 'of', and 'faintly' is the adverb. The second drops 'of'; 'faint' is an adjective; 'smells' disagrees with plural 'letters'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition follows 'smell' when naming the source?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about who she wrote to first.
options: She was the first person I wrote.
She was the first person who I wrote to her.
She was the first person I wrote to.
She was the first person whom I wrote to her.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The first person I wrote to' correctly keeps the preposition at the end. The second drops 'to'; the others repeat the object with 'her'.
mcQuestions.hint: Does the verb 'write to' need 'to', and should the object be repeated?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that says they keep contact.
options: We do not write every week now, but we stay in touched.
We do not write every week now, but we stay on touch.
We not write every week now, but we stay in touch.
We do not write every week now, but we stay in touch.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Stay in touch' is the fixed idiom. 'In touched' is wrong; 'on touch' is the wrong preposition; 'We not write' drops the auxiliary.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the fixed phrase meaning 'keep contact'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence about the late reply is best written?
options: Her reply came three weeks later.
Her reply came three weeks more late.
Her reply came three weeks lately.
Her reply was come three weeks later.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Three weeks later' is the natural time phrase. 'More late' is wrong; 'lately' means 'recently'; 'was come' is ungrammatical.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word means 'after that point in time'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best closing sentence about the friendship.
options: I do not need meet her to know that she is my friend.
I do not need to meet her to know that she is my friend.
I do not need to meeting her to know that she is my friend.
I do not need to meet her for know that she is my friend.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Need to meet... to know' uses the infinitive correctly. 'Need meet' drops 'to'; 'to meeting' is wrong; 'for know' should be 'to know'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'need', which form of the verb is correct?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Can you truly know someone you have never met in person? What does a friendship by letter or screen give you that a face-to-face one might not?
